message:
refactor(export): load sessions from DB instead of JSONL and optimize rendering

HTML session export (ui/app.rs, ui/data.rs):
- Completely rewrite export_session_task() to load conversation data
  from the SQLite database via load_conversation_uncached() instead of
  manually parsing the JSONL source file line by line. This is more
  reliable because the DB contains fully parsed, deduplicated messages
  with correct role enums and timestamps, whereas the raw JSONL parsing
  was fragile (it ignored parse errors, had incomplete content-array
  handling, and lost message ordering metadata).
- The function now accepts a db_path parameter and opens a read-only
  SqliteStorage connection. Messages are mapped from the model's
  MessageRole enum to the HTML exporter's role strings, and timestamps
  are converted from millisecond epoch to RFC 3339 format.
- Message index and author fields are now populated (previously always
  None), enabling richer HTML exports.
- Make load_conversation_uncached() pub(crate) so export_session_task
  can call it from the same crate.
- Update the test for export collision handling to set up a proper mock
  DB with agents, conversations, and messages tables instead of writing
  a temporary JSONL file.

Source chip display (ui/app.rs):
- Fix duplicate host display in search result metadata. The
  source_display_label() helper already incorporates the host when
  present, so the extra "@{host}" suffix was showing the host twice
  (e.g., "remote@myhost@myhost"). Removed the conditional branch.

Theme toggle test (ui/app.rs):
- Fix off-by-one in theme_toggle_stress_does_not_panic: change from 20
  iterations (even, returns to dark) to 19 iterations with corrected
  assertion message, matching the actual theme preset count where an
  odd number of presets means 19 toggles returns to the start.

Markdown export (export.rs):
- Remove redundant backtick wrapping and inner backtick stripping for
  workspace and source path fields in markdown export. The
  escape_markdown() function already handles special characters; the
  extra backtick formatting was incorrectly double-escaping paths
  containing backticks.

HTML renderer (html_export/renderer.rs):
- Add a fast-path byte-length check before the expensive chars().count()
  call in both render_message_group() and render_message(). Since
  byte length is always >= char count for UTF-8, if byte length is
  under the collapse threshold, the char count must also be under it.
  The lazy content_chars calculation only runs when the byte-length
  heuristic suggests collapsing might be needed. This avoids O(n)
  character counting on the majority of messages that are well below
  the threshold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
